description_long: This is a large, robust vase with a cylindrical body that tapers slightly towards the base and a wide, thick-rimmed opening. The exterior is adorned with a combination of geometric sunburst patterns and a central band featuring intricate Celtic knotwork, giving it a decorative and somewhat historical appearance. The surface has a weathered, patinated bronze or copper finish, with greenish oxidation accentuating the engraved details and seams. Two sturdy, metal handles are attached symmetrically on opposite sides, suggesting ease of lifting or moving. On the other two sides, there are rectangular, reinforced slots or brackets, possibly for securing or mounting the vase. The overall construction appears solid and heavy, suitable for outdoor urban settings such as plazas, parks, or entryways.
description: A large, metal vase with sunburst and Celtic knot patterns, greenish patina, and sturdy side handles. Its weathered finish and robust build make it ideal for decorative use in urban outdoor spaces.
description_view_0: This view highlights the ornate sunburst and Celtic knot patterns, as well as both curved metal handles on either side.
description_view_1: This view shows a plain side with a rectangular bracket or slot, lacking the decorative handles seen in other views.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this side displays the sunburst and knotwork patterns with both handles visible, mirroring the opposite side.
description_view_3: This view features the second plain side with a rectangular bracket or slot, again without the decorative handles.
